{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question is whether mossy fibers release GABA. From the provided documents, Document [6] (Neuroanatomy, Cerebellum -- Structure and Function) explicitly states: 'Mossy fibers use glutamate, while the climbing fibers use aspartate as their main excitatory neurotransmitter to provide excitatory signals to the Purkinje cells.' This clearly indicates that mossy fibers release glutamate, not GABA. No document mentions mossy fibers releasing GABA. While GABA is a major inhibitory neurotransmitter in the CNS and is involved in various neural circuits, the specific input to Purkinje cells from mossy fibers is described as excitatory and mediated by glutamate. Therefore, mossy fibers do not release GABA.", "answer_choice": "B"}
